chapter_context.summary: At the second banquet, Queen Esther petitions for her life and the lives of her people, identifying Haman as the enemy; the king orders Haman hanged on the very gallows built for Mordecai.

outline.text: Second Banquet; Queen Esther Petitions the King for Her People's Lives
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Second Banquet; Queen Esther Petitions the King for Her People's Lives (7:1-4), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Esther.

outline.text: Esther Identifies Haman as the Adversary and Enemy
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Esther Identifies Haman as the Adversary and Enemy (7:5-6), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Esther.

outline.text: King's Anger; Haman Hanged on His Own Gallows
outline.explanation: This section focuses on King's Anger; Haman Hanged on His Own Gallows (7:7-10), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Esther.

key_themes.title: Unseen Providence
key_themes.description: Although God is not explicitly mentioned by name, His perfect timing, coordination of details, and deliverance are evident throughout the narrative.

key_themes.title: Courage and Duty
key_themes.description: Esther and Mordecai show that standing up for justice and protecting the vulnerable requires courage and taking personal risks.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 7, how can you recognize God's silent directing in your own life's circumstances?
What does it mean for you to act with courage 'for such a time as this' in your sphere of influence?

cultural_historical.title: The Persian Empire and Purim
cultural_historical.text: The events take place in Susa (Shushan), one of the capital cities of the Achaemenid Empire, and explain the origin of the Feast of Purim.
